title: credibility config + zod schema (per-type half-lives, per-maturity floor, score_breakdown.credibility)
description: Land the SHARED foundation for the content-age credibility multiplier: (a) fabric-config zod fields for per-type half-lives and per-maturity floor, (b) a config-loader reader cloning readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays' per-key validated Map pattern, (c) a new optional `credibility` component on BOTH the score_breakdown zod object (api-contracts.ts:529) AND the RecallScoreBreakdown TS interface (agents.ts:80 — a SEPARATE hand-written declaration TASK-002 emits into; extend it here or tsc fails when TASK-002 adds the key). No scoring logic here — only the config surface + wire schema + type. Ends by rebuilding fabric-shared dist so server/cli can consume the new fields.
action: In packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts (fabricConfigSchema, near the orphan_demote block at :451) add optional per-type half-life fields `credibility_half_life_decisions_days`, `credibility_half_life_guidelines_days`, `credibility_half_life_models_days`, `credibility_half_life_pitfalls_days`, `credibility_half_life_processes_days` each `z.number().int().min(1).max(3650).optional()`, and per-maturity floor fields `credibility_floor_draft`, `credibility_floor_verified`, `credibility_floor_proven` each `z.number().min(0).max(1).optional()`. In packages/server/src/config-loader.ts add `readCredibilityHalfLives(projectRoot)` returning per-type days (defaults decisions 180 / guidelines 150 / models 150 / pitfalls 120 / processes 120) and `readCredibilityFloors(projectRoot)` returning per-maturity floors (defaults draft 0.4 / verified 0.55 / proven 0.7), both cloning the readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays validated-Map best-effort pattern at :231. In packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts add `credibility: z.number().optional()` to the score_breakdown zod object (:529-543). In packages/shared/src/types/agents.ts add `credibility?: number` (optional, additive, comment mirroring the proximity note) to the `RecallScoreBreakdown` interface (:80-94) — this is a hand-written TS interface separate from the zod object, and TASK-002 will emit `credibility` into a RecallScoreBreakdown, so tsc fails if this is not extended. Then `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build`.

criteria: packages/shared/src/types/agents.ts RecallScoreBreakdown interface contains 'credibility?: number' (the hand-written TS interface, separate from the zod object — required or TASK-002's emit fails tsc)
packages/server/src/config-loader.ts contains 'export function readCredibilityHalfLives' and 'export function readCredibilityFloors'
config-loader default values are exactly decisions=180, guidelines=150, models=150, pitfalls=120, processes=120 and floors draft=0.4, verified=0.55, proven=0.7 (grep-visible constants)
readCredibilityHalfLives / readCredibilityFloors return the defaults on a corrupt/missing config (wrapped in try/catch mirroring readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays)
`pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` exits 0 (dist regenerated so the new score_breakdown.credibility field is present in built types)
convergence.verification: node -e "const s=require('fs').readFileSync('packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts','utf8'); process.exit(s.includes('credibility: z.number().optional()')?0:1)"; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: The credibility feature's tunable knobs (per-type half-lives, per-maturity floors) and its wire-visible score component exist as validated config + schema, with dist rebuilt, so the multiplier task can consume them.

files.change: Add 5 optional per-type credibility_half_life_<type>_days fields (int 1..3650) and 3 optional per-maturity credibility_floor_<maturity> fields (number 0..1), near the orphan_demote block at :451.

Two best-effort readers cloning readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays (:231): per-key validate + default fill. Defaults decisions 180 / guidelines 150 / models 150 / pitfalls 120 / processes 120; floors draft 0.4 / verified 0.55 / proven 0.7.
Add `credibility: z.number().optional()` — declared so zod .strip() does not drop it at the MCP boundary (KT-PIT-0005).

Add `credibility?: number` (optional, additive) with a comment mirroring the proximity note. Separate hand-written TS declaration from the zod object; TASK-002 emits `credibility` into a RecallScoreBreakdown so this must exist or tsc fails.

implementation: Read the orphan_demote_*_days block at fabric-config.ts:451-453 and the broad_review_recheck_days field to match style/comments.
Add the 3 credibility_floor_<maturity> fields (draft/verified/proven) as z.number().min(0).max(1).optional().
In api-contracts.ts add `credibility: z.number().optional()` inside the score_breakdown object alongside proximity, with a comment mirroring the proximity note about .strip()/KT-PIT-0005.

In packages/shared/src/types/agents.ts add `credibility?: number` to the RecallScoreBreakdown interface (:80-94) alongside proximity — this is the hand-written TS type TASK-002 emits into; without it TASK-002's `{ ..., credibility }` object literal fails strict tsc.

In config-loader.ts add readCredibilityHalfLives(projectRoot): clone readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays — read the 5 keys, validate each (int 1..3650), default-fill missing/invalid to the per-type defaults, return a full Record<type,number>.
Add readCredibilityFloors(projectRoot): same pattern for the 3 floor keys, validate (number in [0,1]), default-fill draft 0.4 / verified 0.55 / proven 0.7, return Record<maturity,number>.

success_metrics: fabric-shared dist rebuilt with credibility field
readers return defaults on corrupt config

reference.pattern: Per-key validated Map config reader with default fill (readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays), and .strip()-safe score_breakdown component declaration (proximity)

rationale.chosen_approach: Keep config + schema in one task because they are the shared contract the multiplier depends on; both live in fabric-shared/server config and both require the same dist rebuild. Cloning the proven orphan_demote reader guarantees hot-path safety and consistent validation.
decision_factors: shared-schema change forces a dist rebuild — a real build dependency that justifies a foundation task before wiring
the reader defaults ARE the feature's tuning policy, so they belong with the schema, not the scoring code
rationale.tradeoffs: Per-type keys are verbose vs a single nested object; flat keys chosen to mirror the existing orphan_demote_*_days convention and keep the loader identical in shape.

risks: Forgetting the dist rebuild → server/cli consume stale types and score_breakdown.credibility gets stripped at the wire (mitigated by the explicit build step in convergence).
